rulings.Case Title: NICK MINIELLO VS. PACIFIC GAS AND ELECTRIC COMPANY, A CALIFORNIA et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, October 27, 2010, line 4, DEFENDANT PACIFIC GAS AND ELECTRIC COMPANY'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT/SUMMARY ADJUDICATION DENIED. TRIABLE ISSUE OF FACT RAISED WHETHER DEFENDANT ACTED WITH DISCRIMINATORY ANIMUS WHEN TERMINATING PLAINTIFF. EVEN IF PLAINTIFF'S EMPLOYMENT WAS AT-WILL, DEFENDANT CANNOT FIRE PLAINTIFF FOR DISCRIMINATORY REASONS. SEE STEVENSON V. SUPERIOR COURT (1997) 16 CAL.4TH 880, 887. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: ANDREA GORDON VS. THE BAY AREA AIR QUALITY MANAGEMENT DISTRICT et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Quash Service Of Summons
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, October 27, 2010, line 5, DEFENDANT THE BAY AREA AIR QUALITY MANAGEMENT DISTRICT'S Motion To Quash Service Of Summons. GRANTED. UNDER CCP SECTION 415.30, SERVICE IS DEEMED COMPLETE ON THE DAY THE ACKNOWLEDGMENT FORM IS EXECUTED AND RETURNED. WHERE DEFENDANT REFUSES TO SIGN THE ACKNOWLEDGMENT OF RECEIPT, PLAINTIFF MAY ATTEMPT TO EFFECT SERVICE IN SOME OTHER MANNER AND HOLD DEFENDANT LIABLE FOR THE COSTS. CCP SECTION 415.30. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: JOSEPH LEVA et al VS. K2 PARTNERING SOLUTIONS WEST, INC.,A DELAWARE et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice And Motion To Compel Production Of Documents
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, October 27, 2010, line 4, DEFENDANTS EOIN O'TOOLE, K2 PARTNERING SOLUTIONS WEST, INC.'S Motion To Compel Production Of Documents. GRANTED IN ITS ENTIRETY. PLAINTIFFS FAIL TO SHOW THAT THE TAX RETURN PRIVILEGE APPLIES OR THAT THE ITEMS SOUGHT ARE NOT RELEVANT TO THE SUBJECT MATTER OF THE CASE. CCP SECTION 2017.010. FURTHER RESPONSES WITHOUT OBJECTION AND DOCUMENTS ARE DUE WITHIN 30 DAYS OF NOTICE OF THIS ORDER. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO, BY AND THROUGH, VS. CARL ERNST JR
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, October 27, 2010, line 11, PLAINTIFF CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO, MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION. MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION GRANTED IN PART AND DENIED IN PART. MOTION GRANTED AS TO DEFENSES (3)LACHES (SEE NORTHRIDGE HOSP. FOUND. V. PIC N' SAVE NO.9 INC. (1986) 187 CAL.APP.3D 1088); (8)PAYMENT; (10)OFFSET; (19)REPAIR AND DEDUCT; (11)UNJUST ENRICHMENT; (13)FRUSTRATION OF PURPOSE; (16)PORT'S BREACH; (17)ANTICIPATORY REPUDIATION; (18)BREACH OF WARRANTY OF FITNESS FOR A PARTICULAR PURPOSE; (14) EXCUSED PERFORMANCE; (15)PREVENTION OF PERFORMANCE; (22)VOID AS AGAINST PUBLIC POLICY; (23)AMBIQUITY OF CONTRACT; AND (24)SUBSTANTIAL COMPLIANCE. MOST OF THESE DEFENSES MAY BE APPLICABLE TO A BREACH OF CONTRACT ACTION, NOT AN UNLAWFUL DETAINER, AND ARE UNRELATED TO THE ISSUE OF POSSESSION. SOME OF THE DEFENSES, SUCH AS CONTRACT IS AMBIGUOUS, ARE NOT COGNIZABLE. MOTION GRANTED AS TO DEFENSES (25) OTHER ACTION PENDING (REYNOLDS DECLARATION, PARAGRAPH 14) AND (29)LEASE NOT TERMINATED (DEFENSE NOT COGNIZABLE.) MOTION DENIED AS TO REMAINING DEFENSES, FAILURE TO SHIFT THE BURDEN OF PROOF. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: CARITAS MANAGEMENT CORPORATION VS. JEFFREY DONATI, ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiff'S Ex Parte Application For Judgment
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, October 27, 2010, line 12, Plaintiff'S Ex Parte Application For Judgment. GRANTED. DEFENDANT BREACHED THE SETTLEMENT AGREEMENT BY VIOLATING THE VISITORS POLICY WHICH IS INCORPORATED INTO THE AGREEMENT. PLAINTIFF OFFERED A REASONABLE ACCOMMODATION BY GRANTING DEFENDANT'S REQUEST TO HAVE A LIVE-IN AIDE. THAT AIDE, HOWEVER, CANNOT BE DEFENDANT'S WIFE. IT WOULD BE UNREASONABLE FOR PLAINTIFF TO LOSE ITS STATUS IN THE IRS'S LOW INCOME TAX CREDIT PROGRAM AND FOREGO THEIR TAX CREDITS. =(302/CWW)
